title: Quiz: Quantum Neural Networks
description: Test your understanding of QNN architectures and expressibility

questions.question: What is expressibility in the context of quantum neural networks?

options.text: The number of parameters in the circuit
How well the circuit can approximate any quantum state (closeness to Haar-random)

questions.explanation: Expressibility measures how uniformly a parameterized circuit can cover the Hilbert space. High expressibility means the circuit can generate states close to the Haar-random distribution, indicating it can approximate many different quantum states.

questions.question: What does the Meyer-Wallach measure quantify?

options.text: Average entanglement across all qubits in a state

questions.explanation: The Meyer-Wallach measure Q quantifies multipartite entanglement by averaging the linear entropy of single-qubit reduced density matrices. Q=0 for product states and Q=1 for maximally entangled states.

questions.question: In a hardware-efficient ansatz, what is the purpose of the entangling layer?

options.text: To create correlations between qubits that cannot be achieved classically

questions.explanation: Entangling gates (like CNOT) create quantum correlations between qubits. Without entanglement, the circuit would only produce product states, severely limiting expressibility and the ability to learn complex patterns.

questions.question: Why is 'full' entanglement more expressive but potentially problematic?

options.text: It has O(n^2) gates per layer, increasing depth and noise sensitivity

questions.explanation: Full (all-to-all) entanglement connects every qubit pair, giving O(n^2) CNOT gates per layer. While more expressive, this increases circuit depth significantly, making the circuit more susceptible to noise on NISQ devices.

questions.question: A quantum neural network with more parameters is always more expressive.
questions.explanation: False. Expressibility depends on circuit structure, not just parameter count. Redundant parameters or poor architecture can waste parameters without improving expressibility. Additionally, very expressive circuits may suffer from barren plateaus.

questions.question: What is the effective 'hidden layer width' of a QNN with n qubits?
questions.explanation: A QNN with n qubits operates in a 2^n dimensional Hilbert space. This exponential scaling is a key potential advantage of quantum computing - the effective representation power grows exponentially with qubit count.

questions.question: In strongly entangling layers, what makes them different from hardware-efficient ansatze?

options.text: They use shifted CNOT patterns and full rotations (Rx, Ry, Rz) per qubit

questions.explanation: Strongly entangling layers use all three rotation gates (Rx, Ry, Rz) on each qubit and CNOT patterns that shift across layers, ensuring different qubit pairs interact in different layers for maximum expressibility.

questions.question: Which of the following are benefits of tree tensor network ansatze? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Hierarchical structure mimicking classical tensor networks
Efficient classical simulation for certain states
Natural structure for classification (funnels to single output)
Maximum entanglement between all qubit pairs

questions.explanation: Tree tensor ansatze have hierarchical structure inspired by MERA networks, can be efficiently simulated classically for area-law states, and naturally funnel to fewer qubits for output. They don't maximize all pairwise entanglement like full connectivity would.
